task:
Wire up next-intl translations across ALL user-facing pages and components. Currently only HeroSection.tsx and Navbar.tsx call useTranslations — every other page and component (Footer, contact, about, off-plan, buy, rent, search, communities, developers, news, profile, project detail, property detail, list-your-property, signin, forgot-password, and all shared components) has hardcoded English strings.

Goal: every visible UI string that we control (labels, headings, buttons, placeholders, empty states, error messages, form labels) must go through next-intl so it renders correctly in English, Russian, Chinese, and Arabic. DB-sourced content (property names, developer names, project descriptions) stays as-is — only static UI strings.

Admin pages (/admin/*) are excluded — internal tool, English only is fine.

stages.spec:
# Wire next-intl translations across all user-facing pages + components ## Why this will be good End state: a Russian/Chinese/Arabic user lands on any page and reads every static UI string in their language. The site stops feeling half-translated. A future eslint rule blocks anyone from shipping a hardcoded JSX string, so this stays fixed forever. Benchmark: Airbnb's localized site — no English leaks through on tabs, empty states, or validation messages. Production-ready checklist for this task: - All 4 locale files stay in lockstep (next-intl throws at runtime if a key is missing in any locale). - RTL (Arabic) continues to render correctly — layout already handles direction, we only add strings. - Form validation errors (client-side zod / inline JS) translate, not just submit-button labels. - No regressions on existing keys (nav / common / home / search / offPlan / rent / buy / valuation / contact / footer / lang). - eslint-plugin-i18next installed and configured as JSX-only (off for JS string literals to avoid thousands of false positives on imports/URLs/className strings), with a small ignore list for admin pages, test files, api routes. ## Scope — files to touch All paths relative to `/Users/zoop/binayah-properties`. ### Locale files (MUST stay in sync — every new key added to all 4) - `messages/en.json` - `messages/ru.json` - `messages/zh.json` - `messages/ar.json` ### Pages — `src/app/[locale]/...` - `about/page.tsx` — full marketing copy translates - `contact/page.tsx` — full marketing copy translates - `off-plan/page.tsx` - `buy/page.tsx` - `rent/page.tsx` - `search/page.tsx` - `communities/page.tsx` + `communities/[slug]/page.tsx` — UI chrome only on detail page (headings, tabs, buttons, empty states). DB-sourced community descriptions stay as-is. - `developers/page.tsx` + `developers/[slug]/page.tsx` — UI chrome only on detail page. - `news/page.tsx` + `news/[slug]/page.tsx` — UI chrome only on detail (NOT article body). - `project/[slug]/page.tsx` — UI chrome only: Overview / Floor Plans / Location tab labels, buttons, section headings, empty states. DB content (name, description, price, amenities list) stays raw. - `property/[slug]/page.tsx` — same pattern (UI chrome only). - `profile/page.tsx` + `profile/ProfileClient.tsx` (812 lines — biggest page in scope) — tab labels, empty states, edit form labels + validation errors, notifications copy. - `signin/page.tsx` + `signin/SignInClient.tsx` (355 lines) — tab labels, form labels, buttons, client-side validation errors. - `forgot-password/page.tsx` + `forgot-password/ForgotPasswordClient.tsx` — form + validation errors. - `reset-password/page.tsx` + `reset-password/ResetPasswordClient.tsx` — form + validation errors. - `list-your-property/page.tsx` (48 lines — mostly renders ``) — page-level heading/intro. Form strings live in component below. - `pulse/page.tsx` - `valuation/page.tsx` — already has keys for title/subtitle; extend for rest. - `construction-updates/page.tsx` + `construction-updates/[slug]/page.tsx` — UI chrome only. - `services/page.tsx` ### Shared components — `src/components/...` - `Footer.tsx` — extend existing `footer.*` namespace (tagline/quickLinks/services/contact/rights already exist). - `HomePageClient.tsx` — section wrappers, CTAs. - `FavoritesDrawer.tsx` — drawer title, empty state, remove button, cta. - `PropertyActions.tsx` — favorite button tooltip/aria labels. - `InquirySection.tsx` — form labels, success/error states, whatsapp CTA. - `ListPropertyForm.tsx` — all form labels, placeholders, validation errors (zod `.message` strings and any inline JS errors), success message. - `NewsSection.tsx` — section heading, CTA. - `CommunitiesSection.tsx` — section heading, CTA. - `FeaturedProperties.tsx` / `FeaturedPropertiesClient.tsx` / `FeaturedPropertiesServer.tsx` — section heading, filter tabs, empty state, CTA. - `NewsletterStrip.tsx` — copy, input placeholder, button. - `CookieConsent.tsx` — banner copy, accept/decline buttons. - `CryptoBanner.tsx` — already partially keyed under `home.crypto.*`; extend for any remaining strings. - `AIChatWidget.tsx` — prompt suggestions, input placeholder, initial greeting, aria labels. - `AIPulseBanner.tsx` — banner text, CTA. - `SubscribeButton.tsx` — button label, popover copy, success/error states. - `ProjectSubscribeSection.tsx` — headline, 3 benefit bullets, input placeholder, button. - `SavedPropertiesSection.tsx` — section heading, empty state, CTA. - `UserMenu.tsx` — menu items (Profile, Sign out, etc). - `OffPlanSection.tsx` / `OffPlanSectionClient.tsx` / `OffPlanSectionServer.tsx` — section heading, CTA. - `DevelopersSection.tsx` — section heading. - `ServicesSection.tsx` — section heading, item cards. - `StatsSection.tsx` — stat labels. - `TestimonialsSection.tsx` — section heading (testimonial quotes are content, stay raw). - `WhatWeOffer.tsx` — section heading, feature cards. - `ValuationBanner.tsx` / `ValuationCTA.tsx` / `ValuationEntry.tsx` / `ValuationStrip.tsx` — CTA copy. - `MarketDashboard.tsx` — UI chrome (metric labels, tab labels). Raw numbers stay. - `MortgageCalculator.tsx` — input labels, result labels, buttons. - `ROICalculator.tsx` — input labels, result labels, buttons. - `PropertyComparison.tsx` — column headers, empty state, CTA. - `PropertyMatcher.tsx` — prompt copy, buttons, result labels. - `ValuationEntry.tsx` and `components/valuation/*` — form labels, step headings, result copy. - `FAQSection.tsx` — section heading; question/answer text stays raw (CMS-style content). - `Breadcrumbs.tsx` — home label, separators. - `NotificationsBell.tsx` — aria label, empty state, 'mark read' button. - `WhatsAppButton.tsx` — aria label / tooltip. - `ScrollToTop.tsx` — aria label. - `ListYourPropertySection.tsx` — section heading, CTA. ### Config files - `package.json` — add `eslint-plugin-i18next` to `devDependencies`. - Create `.eslintrc.json` at repo root (none exists currently — Next.js uses defaults). Must extend `next/core-web-vitals` (preserve existing lint) and add i18next rule. ## Key naming convention **Namespace per logical surface.** One top-level key per page or component family. Short camelCase names. - Page namespace = route name in camelCase. Examples: `about`, `contact`, `signIn`, `forgotPassword`, `resetPassword`, `profile`, `listProperty`, `projectDetail`, `propertyDetail`, `communities`, `communityDetail`, `developers`, `developerDetail`, `news`, `newsDetail`, `pulse`, `valuation`, `services`, `constructionUpdates`, `constructionUpdateDetail`. - Shared component namespace = component name in camelCase (drop 'Section' suffix when it adds no value). Examples: `footer`, `inquiry`, `favoritesDrawer`, `subscribe`, `newsletter`, `cookieConsent`, `aiChat`, `userMenu`, `featuredProperties`, `mortgageCalculator`, `roiCalculator`, `notificationsBell`. - Nested structure inside a namespace is fine when it helps scannability: ```json "signIn": { "title": "Sign in", "tabs": { "google": "Continue with Google", "email": "Email" }, "form": { "emailLabel": "Email", "passwordLabel": "Password", "submit": "Sign in" }, "errors": { "invalidEmail": "Enter a valid email", "wrongPassword": "Incorrect email or password" } } ``` - **Reuse `common.*` for truly shared words** (Loading, Save, Cancel, Close, Back, Next, Submit, etc). Extend the existing `common` namespace — do NOT duplicate these per-page. Add: `save`, `cancel`, `close`, `back`, `next`, `submit`, `edit`, `delete`, `confirm`, `yes`, `no`, `required`, `optional`, `email`, `phone`, `name`, `message`, `success`, `error`, `tryAgain`. - **Reuse existing namespaces — extend, do not replace:** `nav`, `common`, `home`, `search`, `offPlan`, `rent`, `buy`, `valuation`, `contact`, `footer`, `lang` are already populated and referenced elsewhere. Do not rename or delete existing keys. Only add new keys under these or add new sibling namespaces. - Interpolation: use next-intl's `{placeholder}` syntax (already used in `common.fromPrice` and `search.results`). Keep it for plurals/counts. ## Server vs client pattern **Client components (`"use client"` at top):** ```tsx import { useTranslations } from "next-intl"; export default function Foo() { const t = useTranslations("foo"); return

{t("title")}

; } ``` **Server components (no `"use client"`, including `async` page.tsx files):** ```tsx import { getTranslations } from "next-intl/server"; export default async function Page() { const t = await getTranslations("foo"); return

{t("title")}

; } ``` **Rules:** - Check for `"use client"` at the top of each file before choosing which hook to use. - For page.tsx files that are thin wrappers around a Client component (signin, forgot-password, reset-password, profile, list-your-property), the page.tsx may stay server-side — translate only its own JSX (rare) and let the Client component handle its own strings via `useTranslations`. - Use one namespace per component — easier to keep scoped. Multi-namespace use inside one component is allowed: call `useTranslations("common")` and `useTranslations("signIn")` as separate hooks. - **For zod validation messages used inside client form components,** build the schema INSIDE the component body so it can capture `t`: ```tsx const t = useTranslations("signIn.errors"); const schema = useMemo(() => z.object({ email: z.string().email(t("invalidEmail")), password: z.string().min(8, t("passwordTooShort")), }), [t]); ``` Never translate schema messages outside render — they'd be baked in at module load before the locale is known. - **For inline JS error strings (toast, alert, throw Error).** Use `t("...")` the same way. If the error will be shown to the user via `toast.error(...)` or state, it's in scope. Errors thrown to the console or to server logs are NOT in scope. ## en.json — which sections to extend vs create new ### Extend (do NOT rename / delete existing keys) - `nav` — already complete, no changes expected unless a new nav link lands. - `common` — add shared action verbs + validation primitives listed above. - `home` — extend for HomePageClient.tsx and any section components it wraps (FeaturedProperties, CommunitiesSection, NewsSection, DevelopersSection, ServicesSection, StatsSection, TestimonialsSection, WhatWeOffer, CryptoBanner, ValuationStrip, OffPlanSection, ListYourPropertySection, AIPulseBanner). Nest under `home.sections.*` when useful (e.g. `home.sections.featured.title`). - `search` — extend for any missing filter/sort/result strings on `search/page.tsx`. - `offPlan`, `buy`, `rent` — extend for the page body beyond title/subtitle (filter bar, result count, sort labels, empty state). - `valuation` — extend for the full page (form steps, result page, CTA). - `contact` — extend for the full page marketing copy + form success/error messages. - `footer` — extend for any missing link labels / disclaimers. ### Create new top-level namespaces - `about` - `profile` (with nested `tabs`, `saved`, `submissions`, `subscriptions`, `edit`, `errors`) - `signIn` (with `tabs`, `form`, `errors`) - `forgotPassword` (with `form`, `errors`, `successMessage`) - `resetPassword` (with `form`, `errors`) - `listProperty` (with `form`, `errors`, `success`) - `projectDetail` (with `tabs`: overview/floorPlans/location, section headings, CTA labels, empty states) - `propertyDetail` - `communities` (list) + `communityDetail` - `developers` (list) + `developerDetail` - `news` (list) + `newsDetail` - `pulse` - `services` - `constructionUpdates` (list) + `constructionUpdateDetail` - `footer` already exists, so components like `NewsletterStrip`, `CookieConsent`, `AIChatWidget`, `AIPulseBanner`, `SubscribeButton`, `ProjectSubscribeSection`, `FavoritesDrawer`, `PropertyActions`, `UserMenu`, `NotificationsBell`, `InquirySection`, `MortgageCalculator`, `ROICalculator`, `PropertyComparison`, `PropertyMatcher`, `MarketDashboard`, `Breadcrumbs`, `SavedPropertiesSection`, `FAQSection`, `WhatsAppButton` each get their own namespace (lowercase-camel as listed above). ## Translation strategy — DEV responsibility - DEV writes first-pass translations for all 4 locales. Machine-translation quality is explicitly acceptable for this task (a native-speaker review pass is scheduled separately). - Recommended approach: write en.json first with all new keys, then batch-translate to ru/zh/ar using an LLM pass. Review proper nouns — 'Dubai', 'Binayah', 'AED' — stay as-is or use established transliterations. Do NOT translate brand name 'Binayah'. - **All 4 files MUST have identical key trees.** Missing keys in ru/zh/ar cause next-intl to throw at runtime. A final sanity check is part of DEV's self-report: `jq -r 'paths(scalars) | join(".")' messages/en.json | sort > /tmp/en.keys && jq -r 'paths(scalars) | join(".")' messages/ar.json | sort > /tmp/ar.keys && diff /tmp/en.keys /tmp/ar.keys` — must return empty. Repeat for ru and zh. - Arabic strings must remain logically readable — don't just copy the English characters. RTL layout is handled at layout level; only the text content changes. ## eslint-plugin-i18next config (FINAL step — do last) After all wiring is complete AND `npm run build` passes AND `npx tsc --noEmit` passes, add the lint rule. 1. `npm install --save-dev eslint-plugin-i18next` 2. Create `/Users/zoop/binayah-properties/.eslintrc.json`: ```json { "extends": ["next/core-web-vitals"], "plugins": ["i18next"], "rules": { "i18next/no-literal-string": ["error", { "mode": "jsx-text-only", "message": "Hardcoded JSX text — wrap in t('key') via useTranslations or getTranslations." }] }, "overrides": [ { "files": ["src/app/**/admin/**/*.tsx", "src/app/**/admin/**/*.ts"], "rules": { "i18next/no-literal-string": "off" } }, { "files": ["src/app/api/**/*.ts", "src/app/**/route.ts", "src/middleware.ts", "src/i18n/**/*.ts", "src/lib/**/*.ts", "src/hooks/**/*.ts", "src/types/**/*.ts", "src/data/**/*.ts", "src/navigation.ts"], "rules": { "i18next/no-literal-string": "off" } }, { "files": ["**/*.test.*", "**/*.spec.*"], "rules": { "i18next/no-literal-string": "off" } } ] } ``` 3. Run `npm run lint` — zero i18next errors expected. If errors remain, fix them (they are real hardcoded strings you missed). 4. If the `jsx-text-only` mode name is not supported in the installed plugin version, fall back to standard mode with these overrides to silence non-JSX cases: ```json "i18next/no-literal-string": ["error", { "markupOnly": true, "onlyAttribute": [], "ignore": ["use client", "use server"], "ignoreCallee": ["useTranslations", "getTranslations", "z.string", "z.object", "cn", "cva", "clsx"], "ignoreAttribute": ["className", "style", "src", "href", "alt", "type", "id", "key", "name", "role", "aria-label", "aria-labelledby", "aria-describedby", "data-testid", "rel", "target", "method", "action", "encType", "autoComplete", "inputMode", "pattern", "placeholder"], "ignoreProperty": ["className", "style", "src", "href", "alt", "type", "id", "key", "name", "role", "data-testid", "rel", "target", "method", "action", "encType", "autoComplete", "inputMode", "pattern"] }] ``` Note: `placeholder` is listed in `ignoreAttribute` because it's valid to have `placeholder={t('...')}` — the lint rule's job is to flag the raw string 'Enter your email' not the attribute itself. Pick the config mode that flags JSX text children and `children={"..."}` while ignoring pure technical attrs. ## Constraints (do not violate) - Do NOT touch `/admin/*` routes or `AdminHeader.tsx` / `InquiriesMobileList.tsx` / `SubmissionsMobileList.tsx` / `SubscriptionsMobileList.tsx`. Admin is English-only by design. - Do NOT translate DB content: property names, descriptions, developer bios, news article bodies (only news page chrome), community descriptions, agent names, addresses. - Do NOT translate SEO meta (`title`, `description` in `generateMetadata`) unless it already uses `getTranslations`. Out of scope. - Do NOT add new features, redesign, refactor architecture, or rename existing translation keys. Extraction-only. - Do NOT break the existing `HeroSection.tsx` / `Navbar.tsx` keys — they already work. - Preserve RTL — check layout in Arabic locale after shipping (no CSS changes needed; just make sure Arabic strings exist for every key). - Brand name 'Binayah' stays as-is in all locales. - All 4 locale JSON files must parse as valid JSON (trailing commas will break build). - No `any` on new props. No new `console.log` calls. ## Acceptance criteria 1. `grep -r 'useTranslations\|getTranslations' src/app/\[locale\] src/components | wc -l` shows a large increase vs current baseline (current: only 2 files). 2. Every file listed above imports either `useTranslations` (client) or `getTranslations` (server) and renders at least one key via `t(...)`. 3. `jq -r 'paths(scalars) | join(".")' messages/en.json | sort` is IDENTICAL to the same command on ru.json, zh.json, ar.json (diff returns empty). 4. `npm run build` passes. 5. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes. 6. Manual smoke: visit `/ru`, `/zh`, `/ar` for about, contact, signin, profile, project/[slug], property/[slug], list-your-property — no raw English leaks in static UI chrome. DB content still in English is EXPECTED and fine. 7. Arabic (RTL) pages render without layout breakage. 8. `npm run lint` runs successfully — zero `i18next/no-literal-string` errors remain (they all got wrapped). Lint must still pass on admin + API routes thanks to overrides. 9. `package.json` has `eslint-plugin-i18next` in `devDependencies`. 10. `.eslintrc.json` exists at repo root and extends `next/core-web-vitals`. 11. No existing en.json keys removed or renamed (git diff on messages/en.json shows additions only, except possibly reordering within objects — fine). ## Work order (suggested, single commit) 1. Define the full en.json key tree first — add every namespace + key you'll need. 2. Batch-translate en.json → ru.json, zh.json, ar.json. Run the key-diff check. 3. Wire components bottom-up: shared components first (Footer, NewsletterStrip, CookieConsent, FavoritesDrawer, UserMenu, NotificationsBell, SubscribeButton, ProjectSubscribeSection, InquirySection, AIChatWidget, etc), then section components (FeaturedProperties, CommunitiesSection, NewsSection, StatsSection, WhatWeOffer, ServicesSection, OffPlanSection, etc), then pages. 4. Pages in increasing complexity: about → contact → off-plan/buy/rent/search → communities/developers/news (list then detail) → project/[slug] → property/[slug] → forgot-password/reset-password → signin → list-your-property → profile. 5. Run `npm run build && npx tsc --noEmit` — fix type/build errors. 6. Manual smoke on `/ru`, `/ar` (RTL) for a few key surfaces. 7. LAST: install eslint-plugin-i18next, add config, run `npm run lint`, fix remaining violations, re-run lint until clean. 8. Commit. ## Demo path 1. Visit `http://localhost:3000/ru` — homepage renders in Russian, all section headings translate. 2. Click any nav link (buy, rent, off-plan, search) — destination page renders in Russian. 3. Visit `/ar/about` — About page renders in Arabic, RTL layout intact, marketing copy readable. 4. Visit `/zh/signin` — Sign-in page tab labels, form labels, submit button all in Chinese. Type invalid email, click submit — validation error in Chinese. 5. Sign in, visit `/ru/profile` — tab labels, empty states, edit form labels in Russian. 6. Visit `/zh/project/sensia-by-beyond` — Overview/Floor Plans/Location tabs in Chinese; project name/description stays English (expected — DB content). 7. Switch back to `/en/*` — zero regressions, everything still renders as before. 8. Add a hardcoded JSX string in any tsx file (e.g. `

Hello world

`), run `npm run lint` — rule fires.

plannerOutput.taskSummary: Wire next-intl translations across every user-facing page and component in the binayah-properties Next.js app. Only HeroSection.tsx and Navbar.tsx currently use translations — all other pages and shared components have hardcoded English strings. Every visible static UI string (labels, headings, buttons, placeholders, empty states, error messages, form validation messages) must be extracted into all 4 locale files (en/ru/zh/ar) and rendered via t(). DB-sourced content (property names, descriptions, developer bios, article bodies, community copy) stays untranslated.

pages: about/page.tsx — full marketing copy included (hardcoded, not DB-driven)
communities/[slug]/page.tsx (detail — UI chrome only: headings, tabs, buttons, empty states)
news/[slug]/page.tsx (detail — UI chrome only, not article body)
project/[slug]/page.tsx (detail — UI chrome only: Overview, Floor Plans, Location tabs, buttons, labels)
profile/page.tsx — including form validation error messages
signin/page.tsx — including form validation error messages
forgot-password/page.tsx — including form validation error messages
reset-password/page.tsx — including form validation error messages
list-your-property/page.tsx — including form validation error messages
confirmedScope.translationStrategy: DEV writes first-pass translations for all 4 languages for every new key (machine-translation quality acceptable; to be reviewed by native speakers later)
confirmedScope.errorMessages: Form validation error messages are in scope — they are visible UI strings

designDecisions.detailPages: Translate UI chrome only (tab labels, button text, section headings like Overview/Floor Plans/Location, empty states). Dynamic DB content (property descriptions, developer bios, article bodies, community descriptions) stays as English.
designDecisions.marketingCopy: about/page.tsx and contact/page.tsx marketing copy is hardcoded, therefore translate it in full.
designDecisions.unlisted_pages: pulse, valuation, construction-updates, and services pages are in scope — task said ALL user-facing pages.
designDecisions.formValidation: All inline JS error strings in forms (signin, forgot-password, reset-password, list-your-property, profile) must go through t() — they are visible to users.
designDecisions.translations_who: DEV writes first-pass translations. Quality: reasonable approximations / MT is fine for now.
designDecisions.eslintPlugin: After all translation work is done, add eslint-plugin-i18next with rule i18next/no-literal-string: error in JSX-only mode to prevent regression. This is the final step of the task.

pattern.serverComponents: const t = await getTranslations('namespace') then t('key')
pattern.namespacing: One namespace per page or logical section (e.g. about, profile, signIn, listProperty, projectDetail, propertyDetail, communities, developers, news, pulse, valuation, services, constructionUpdates)
pattern.localeFiles: messages/en.json, messages/ru.json, messages/zh.json, messages/ar.json — all must be kept in sync, no key missing from any file

constraints: Existing keys in en.json (nav, common, home, search, offPlan, rent, buy, valuation, contact, footer, lang) must not be broken — only extend.
All 4 locale files must be in sync at all times — next-intl throws at runtime if a key is present in en.json but missing in another locale file.
RTL layout for Arabic is already handled in layout.tsx — no layout changes needed.
Profile page (~812 lines) and SignIn (~355 lines) are the heaviest client components and will have the most keys — budget time accordingly.
Do not touch /admin/* routes — English-only is intentional.
outOfScope: DB-sourced content: property descriptions, project descriptions, developer bios, news article bodies, community descriptions, agent names, addresses from DB
Meta tags and SEO strings (open graph, page <title> unless already using t())
Any new UI features or redesigns — this is extraction-only
gotchas: construction-updates/ and services/ pages exist in the codebase but were not in the original task list — confirm their exact paths before wiring.
next-intl will throw at runtime (not build time) if a key exists in en.json but is absent from ru/zh/ar — always add to all 4 files together.
Some components are imported across multiple pages — wiring a shared component once fixes multiple pages; don't double-count work.
AIChatWidget and AIPulseBanner may have conditional rendering logic — make sure t() calls are not placed outside the component render path.

FINAL STEP (do not skip): add eslint-plugin-i18next with i18next/no-literal-string: error (JSX mode) to .eslintrc so future pages cannot ship hardcoded strings. Run eslint --fix to verify no new violations are introduced by the translation wiring itself.

plan:
# Wire next-intl translations across all user-facing pages + components ## Why this will be good End state: a Russian/Chinese/Arabic user lands on any page and reads every static UI string in their language. The site stops feeling half-translated. A future eslint rule blocks anyone from shipping a hardcoded JSX string, so this stays fixed forever. Benchmark: Airbnb's localized site — no English leaks through on tabs, empty states, or validation messages. Production-ready checklist for this task: - All 4 locale files stay in lockstep (next-intl throws at runtime if a key is missing in any locale). - RTL (Arabic) continues to render correctly — layout already handles direction, we only add strings. - Form validation errors (client-side zod / inline JS) translate, not just submit-button labels. - No regressions on existing keys (nav / common / home / search / offPlan / rent / buy / valuation / contact / footer / lang). - eslint-plugin-i18next installed and configured as JSX-only (off for JS string literals to avoid thousands of false positives on imports/URLs/className strings), with a small ignore list for admin pages, test files, api routes. ## Scope — files to touch All paths relative to `/Users/zoop/binayah-properties`. ### Locale files (MUST stay in sync — every new key added to all 4) - `messages/en.json` - `messages/ru.json` - `messages/zh.json` - `messages/ar.json` ### Pages — `src/app/[locale]/...` - `about/page.tsx` — full marketing copy translates - `contact/page.tsx` — full marketing copy translates - `off-plan/page.tsx` - `buy/page.tsx` - `rent/page.tsx` - `search/page.tsx` - `communities/page.tsx` + `communities/[slug]/page.tsx` — UI chrome only on detail page (headings, tabs, buttons, empty states). DB-sourced community descriptions stay as-is. - `developers/page.tsx` + `developers/[slug]/page.tsx` — UI chrome only on detail page. - `news/page.tsx` + `news/[slug]/page.tsx` — UI chrome only on detail (NOT article body). - `project/[slug]/page.tsx` — UI chrome only: Overview / Floor Plans / Location tab labels, buttons, section headings, empty states. DB content (name, description, price, amenities list) stays raw. - `property/[slug]/page.tsx` — same pattern (UI chrome only). - `profile/page.tsx` + `profile/ProfileClient.tsx` (812 lines — biggest page in scope) — tab labels, empty states, edit form labels + validation errors, notifications copy. - `signin/page.tsx` + `signin/SignInClient.tsx` (355 lines) — tab labels, form labels, buttons, client-side validation errors. - `forgot-password/page.tsx` + `forgot-password/ForgotPasswordClient.tsx` — form + validation errors. - `reset-password/page.tsx` + `reset-password/ResetPasswordClient.tsx` — form + validation errors. - `list-your-property/page.tsx` (48 lines — mostly renders ``) — page-level heading/intro. Form strings live in component below. - `pulse/page.tsx` - `valuation/page.tsx` — already has keys for title/subtitle; extend for rest. - `construction-updates/page.tsx` + `construction-updates/[slug]/page.tsx` — UI chrome only. - `services/page.tsx` ### Shared components — `src/components/...` - `Footer.tsx` — extend existing `footer.*` namespace. - `HomePageClient.tsx` — section wrappers, CTAs. - `FavoritesDrawer.tsx` — drawer title, empty state, remove button, cta. - `PropertyActions.tsx` — favorite button tooltip/aria labels. - `InquirySection.tsx` — form labels, success/error states, whatsapp CTA. - `ListPropertyForm.tsx` — all form labels, placeholders, validation errors, success message. - `NewsSection.tsx`, `CommunitiesSection.tsx`, `DevelopersSection.tsx`, `ServicesSection.tsx`, `StatsSection.tsx`, `TestimonialsSection.tsx`, `WhatWeOffer.tsx`, `OffPlanSection*.tsx`, `FeaturedProperties*.tsx`, `ValuationBanner.tsx` / `ValuationCTA.tsx` / `ValuationEntry.tsx` / `ValuationStrip.tsx`, `ListYourPropertySection.tsx`, `CryptoBanner.tsx`, `AIPulseBanner.tsx` — section headings, CTAs, filter tabs, empty states. - `NewsletterStrip.tsx`, `CookieConsent.tsx`, `AIChatWidget.tsx`, `SubscribeButton.tsx`, `ProjectSubscribeSection.tsx`, `SavedPropertiesSection.tsx`, `UserMenu.tsx`, `NotificationsBell.tsx`, `WhatsAppButton.tsx`, `ScrollToTop.tsx`, `FAQSection.tsx`, `Breadcrumbs.tsx` — all user-visible copy. - `MortgageCalculator.tsx`, `ROICalculator.tsx`, `PropertyComparison.tsx`, `PropertyMatcher.tsx`, `MarketDashboard.tsx`, `components/valuation/*` — input labels, result labels, buttons. ### Config files - `package.json` — add `eslint-plugin-i18next` to `devDependencies`. - Create `/Users/zoop/binayah-properties/.eslintrc.json` at repo root (none exists currently — Next.js uses defaults). Must extend `next/core-web-vitals` and add i18next rule. ## Key naming convention **Namespace per logical surface.** One top-level key per page or component family. Short camelCase names. - Page namespace = route name in camelCase: `about`, `contact`, `signIn`, `forgotPassword`, `resetPassword`, `profile`, `listProperty`, `projectDetail`, `propertyDetail`, `communities`, `communityDetail`, `developers`, `developerDetail`, `news`, `newsDetail`, `pulse`, `valuation`, `services`, `constructionUpdates`, `constructionUpdateDetail`. - Shared component namespace = component name in camelCase: `footer`, `inquiry`, `favoritesDrawer`, `subscribe`, `newsletter`, `cookieConsent`, `aiChat`, `userMenu`, `featuredProperties`, `mortgageCalculator`, `roiCalculator`, `notificationsBell`, `breadcrumbs`, `propertyActions`, `savedProperties`, `faq`. - Nested keys inside a namespace are fine and encouraged (e.g. `signIn.form.emailLabel`, `signIn.errors.invalidEmail`, `profile.tabs.saved`). - Reuse `common.*` for shared action verbs (Save, Cancel, Close, Back, Next, Submit, Edit, Delete, Confirm, Yes, No, Required, Optional, Email, Phone, Name, Message, Success, Error, Try again). Extend the existing `common` namespace — do NOT duplicate these per-page. - **Extend, do not replace,** existing namespaces: `nav`, `common`, `home`, `search`, `offPlan`, `rent`, `buy`, `valuation`, `contact`, `footer`, `lang`. Don't rename or delete any existing key. - Use next-intl interpolation `{placeholder}` for counts/dynamic values. ## Server vs client pattern **Client components** (`"use client"` at top): ```tsx import { useTranslations } from "next-intl"; const t = useTranslations("foo"); // then: {t("title")} ``` **Server components** (no `"use client"`, async page.tsx files): ```tsx import { getTranslations } from "next-intl/server"; const t = await getTranslations("foo"); // then: {t("title")} ``` Check for `"use client"` at the top of each file before choosing. Multiple `useTranslations` calls per component are allowed (one per namespace). **zod schemas used inside client form components must be built inside the component body so they can capture `t`:** ```tsx const t = useTranslations("signIn.errors"); const schema = useMemo(() => z.object({ email: z.string().email(t("invalidEmail")), }), [t]); ``` Inline JS error strings shown to users (toast, alert, state) translate via `t()`. Console/server logs stay as-is. ## en.json — extend vs create new **Extend existing (no renames/deletes):** `nav`, `common`, `home` (nest under `home.sections.*` for section components), `search`, `offPlan`, `rent`, `buy`, `valuation`, `contact`, `footer`. **Create new top-level namespaces:** `about`, `profile`, `signIn`, `forgotPassword`, `resetPassword`, `listProperty`, `projectDetail`, `propertyDetail`, `communities`, `communityDetail`, `developers`, `developerDetail`, `news`, `newsDetail`, `pulse`, `services`, `constructionUpdates`, `constructionUpdateDetail`, plus one namespace per shared component as listed above. ## Translation strategy - DEV writes first-pass translations for all 4 locales. MT quality acceptable. - Write en.json first, then batch-translate to ru/zh/ar. - Brand name 'Binayah' stays as-is. 'Dubai', 'AED' — use established transliterations. - Final sanity check (run before committing): ``` jq -r 'paths(scalars) | join(".")' messages/en.json | sort > /tmp/en.keys for l in ru zh ar; do jq -r 'paths(scalars) | join(".")' messages/$l.json | sort > /tmp/$l.keys diff /tmp/en.keys /tmp/$l.keys || echo "MISMATCH: $l" done ``` Must return empty for all 3 locales. ## eslint-plugin-i18next — FINAL step After all wiring done AND `npm run build` AND `npx tsc --noEmit` pass: 1. `npm install --save-dev eslint-plugin-i18next` 2. Create `.eslintrc.json` at repo root: ```json { "extends": ["next/core-web-vitals"], "plugins": ["i18next"], "rules": { "i18next/no-literal-string": ["error", { "markupOnly": true, "ignoreCallee": ["useTranslations", "getTranslations", "z.string", "z.object", "z.enum", "cn", "cva", "clsx", "twMerge"], "ignoreAttribute": ["className", "style", "src", "href", "alt", "type", "id", "key", "name", "role", "aria-label", "aria-labelledby", "aria-describedby", "data-testid", "rel", "target", "method", "action", "encType", "autoComplete", "inputMode", "pattern", "placeholder", "defaultValue"], "ignoreProperty": ["className", "style", "src", "href", "alt", "type", "id", "key", "name", "role", "data-testid", "rel", "target", "method", "action", "encType", "autoComplete", "inputMode", "pattern"] }] }, "overrides": [ { "files": ["src/app/**/admin/**/*.{ts,tsx}"], "rules": { "i18next/no-literal-string": "off" } }, { "files": ["src/app/api/**/*.ts", "src/app/**/route.ts", "src/middleware.ts", "src/i18n/**/*.ts", "src/lib/**/*.ts", "src/hooks/**/*.ts", "src/types/**/*.ts", "src/data/**/*.ts", "src/navigation.ts"], "rules": { "i18next/no-literal-string": "off" } }, { "files": ["**/*.test.*", "**/*.spec.*"], "rules": { "i18next/no-literal-string": "off" } } ] } ``` 3. `npm run lint` — zero i18next errors. Fix any real hardcoded strings the rule catches. ## Constraints - Do NOT touch `/admin/*` routes or the admin mobile list components. - Do NOT translate DB content: property/project/community/developer names, descriptions, agent names, addresses, article bodies. - Do NOT translate SEO metadata (`generateMetadata` return) unless it already uses `getTranslations`. - Do NOT rename, delete, or restructure existing translation keys — extend only. - No new features, no redesigns, no refactors. Extraction only. - No `any` on new props. No new `console.log` calls. ## Acceptance criteria 1. Every file listed above imports `useTranslations` or `getTranslations` and renders at least one `t(...)` call. 2. `jq -r 'paths(scalars) | join(".")' messages/en.json | sort` matches ru.json / zh.json / ar.json exactly (diff returns empty for all 3). 3. `npm run build` passes. 4. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes. 5. Smoke-test `/ru`, `/zh`, `/ar` on about, contact, signin, profile, project/sensia-by-beyond, property/[any], list-your-property — no raw English leaks in static UI chrome. RTL intact on `/ar`. 6. `npm run lint` passes with `eslint-plugin-i18next` installed and configured — zero `i18next/no-literal-string` errors remaining. 7. `package.json` has `eslint-plugin-i18next` in `devDependencies`. `.eslintrc.json` exists at repo root. 8. No existing en.json keys removed/renamed (git diff shows additions only). ## Work order (single commit) 1. Build the full en.json key tree (every namespace + key planned). 2. Batch-translate en.json → ru/zh/ar. Run key-diff check. 3. Wire shared components first, then page.tsx files in order: about → contact → off-plan/buy/rent/search → communities/developers/news → project/[slug] → property/[slug] → forgot-password/reset-password → signin → list-your-property → profile → pulse/valuation/services/construction-updates. 4. Run `npm run build && npx tsc --noEmit`. 5. Manual smoke on `/ru` and `/ar`. 6. LAST: install eslint-plugin-i18next, add config, run `npm run lint`, fix violations. 7. Commit. ## Demo path 1. `http://localhost:3000/ru` → homepage in Russian, all sections translate. 2. Nav to `/ru/buy`, `/ru/rent`, `/ru/off-plan` — all chrome in Russian. 3. `/ar/about` → Arabic, RTL intact, marketing copy readable. 4. `/zh/signin` → Chinese tabs/form/buttons; submit empty form → validation errors in Chinese. 5. Auth seed, `/ru/profile` → tabs + empty states + edit form in Russian. 6. `/zh/project/sensia-by-beyond` → tabs/buttons in Chinese; project name/description stay English (expected, DB content). 7. Back to `/en/*` → zero regressions. 8. Add `

Hello world

` to any tsx file, `npm run lint` → rule fires.
summary: Single-stage extraction task: wire next-intl across all non-admin pages and shared components, keep 4 locale files in sync, then install eslint-plugin-i18next with JSX-text-only rule to prevent regression.

loopContext.designRouteReason: Pure extraction/wiring task — no visual decisions. User explicitly requested skipping designer.

contextGaps.missing: No root .eslintrc / eslint.config at repo root. Next.js 15 uses its built-in defaults via `next lint`. To add custom rules (e.g. i18next plugin), create `.eslintrc.json` at repo root extending `next/core-web-vitals`.

next-intl messages live at `messages/{en,ru,zh,ar}.json` (4 locales, 105 lines each currently). `src/i18n/request.ts` imports them dynamically from `../../messages/${locale}.json`. All locale files must have identical key trees or next-intl throws at runtime when rendering a missing key.
As of this task only HeroSection.tsx and Navbar.tsx use `useTranslations`. Every other page/component hardcodes English — which this task fixes.
